rows.notes: Top-right short phrase reads 'Brought forward' (script common in ledgers). The three amount columns at the top are clearly aligned; digits read as 676 | 4 | 1. Ink is slightly faded but digits are distinct and within valid ranges.
Description legible as '1 years Income Tax'. Amount columns show '56' in pounds column and '15' in shillings; there is no visible pence digit so pence set to 0. Vertical ruling alignment clear.
Description reads 'Tradesmen's bills viz Hayes'. Amounts read clearly in the three small columns as 16 | 19 | 6; all within valid ranges and well aligned to the ruling lines.
Surname looks like 'Vincent' and second word 'Herald' (both legible). Amounts show 3 | 3 | (no pence) — pence set to 0 due to blank; alignment clear.

Description reads as 'Gib & Ward' (handwriting slightly compressed). Pounds and shillings columns read 49 and 19 clearly; the pence digit appears as a small '9' but is faint — chosen as 9 based on alignment with pence column.

Single-name entry 'Evans' clear. Amount digits appear as '38' in pounds; shillings column looks like '1' and pence like '10' (the '10' is slightly cramped but fits the pence column). Moderate uncertainty due to cramped pence.
Name reads 'Dudley & Bowler' (letters legible). Amounts read clearly as 19 | 4 | 5 with good alignment to ruling lines.
Surname appears as 'Nunney' (script a little stylized). Pounds and shillings read 24 and 14; pence column blank so set to 0. Columns aligned; shillings within valid range.
Name reads 'Watts' clearly. Amounts 30 | 5 | 4 are legible and align with ruling lines; no ambiguity.
Name 'Baxter' clear. Pounds and shillings read 11 and 1; pence appears blank in the pence column so set to 0. Vertical alignment clear.
Name 'Cripps' legible. Amounts appear distinctly as 6 | 3 | 6; digits are clear and pass currency validation.
Surname reads as 'Knowles'. Amounts in the three columns read 13 | 14 | 2; slight ink feathering but digits align correctly and are within valid ranges.
Name 'Roach' clear. Amounts read 5 | 10 | (blank pence). Pence set to 0; column alignment unambiguous.
Description 'Parker Bookseller' clearly readable. Amount columns show 27 | 1 | 6 distinctly and with clean alignment — high confidence.
First name 'Herbert' legible; second word looks like 'Inkling' or similar (handwriting slightly ambiguous). Amounts read 3 | 3 | blank (pence 0). Moderate uncertainty on second word only.
Surname 'Hickman' readable. Amounts appear as 2 | 17 | (blank pence). Shillings 17 valid; pence set to 0 due to blank column.
Name 'Spiers' visible though tail of script is stylized. Amounts read 4 | 17 | blank; pence set to 0. Slight ink fade on shillings but alignment supports reading.
Label 'Common Room' clear. Amounts in columns distinctly read 14 | 18 | 4 with strong alignment to the ruling lines.
Name appears as 'Purdee' or 'Purdue' — chosen 'Purdue' as closest readable form. Amounts read 8 | 5 | 2; digits are clear though the name has slight ambiguity.
Names 'Lambert & Rawlings' are legible. Amounts read 3 | 8 | 6 clearly in their columns; alignment is consistent.
First name 'Shrimpton' clear; second phrase reads like 'Prize Books'. Amounts 40 | 10 | blank (pence 0) are legible and aligned.

Description is less clear — appears to refer to care of the stable ('...of Stable' visible). Amount in pounds column reads clearly as 10; shillings/pence columns blank. Moderate uncertainty on exact wording of description.
Phrase reads 'Rectors Improved Stipend' (script clear). Amount is a round 100 in the pounds column; shillings and pence blank — interpreted as 0.
'Do Do' indicates repetition of previous heading; rest reads 'allowance for Kitchen'. Pounds column reads 10 clearly; lesser columns blank. Moderate confidence due to abbreviated header.
Abbreviated 'Do' followed by 'Postage & Parcels' readable. Amount columns show 2 | 12 | 4 distinctly; digits within valid ranges and well aligned.

rows.description: Compensation to Fellows for dry piece
rows.notes: Text reads 'Compensation to Fellows' clearly; final words are harder to read — interpreted as 'for dry piece' with some uncertainty. Pounds read 23 clearly; smaller columns blank.
Phrase appears to be 'Balance on Poor Tax Act' (some letters faint). Amounts read as 11 | 15 | 4; digits aligned but description has moderate ambiguity in one word ('Poor' vs 'Pood').
Single surname 'Browning' readable. Amounts 5 | 16 | 6 are distinct and align with the three ruling columns.

Last descriptive line is heavily abbreviated and faint; I read fragments 'agency', '2 yrs 1843.44' and a closing note possibly 'Returned'. Amounts read as 2 | 2 | blank. Low confidence due to heavy abbreviation and faded script.

Final boxed total at bottom-right clearly visible as 1212 | 12 | (blank pence). The box and line beneath confirm this is the final total. Description column is blank on the line — treated as a total row.
